name: Latvian
description_short: Member-owned credit union in Minneapolis serving the Latvian community for 60+ years, offering competitive rates on loans, savings accounts, and financial services with personalized service.
description_long:
Latvian Credit Union has been operating for over 60 years, establishing itself as a community-focused financial institution rooted in the Minneapolis Latvian community. The credit union was founded to serve members of Latvian organizations and their families, creating a niche institution with deep cultural ties and community identity. The organization remains committed to its founding mission while expanding financial services to meet modern banking needs.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of financial products including car loans, home loans, recreational vehicle loans, personal loans, and student loans, all advertised with competitive rates. On the savings and checking side, they provide multiple savings account options, interest-bearing checking accounts with no minimum balance requirement, teen debit accounts with VISA access to 24,000 surcharge-free ATMs, and remote deposit capabilities through mobile app functionality. Additional services include wire transfers, notary public services, loan payment processing, bill pay, and various financial calculators.

What distinguishes Latvian Credit Union is its emphasis on personalized, relationship-based banking. The organization explicitly markets itself on providing "good, old fashioned service" with real people who "go the extra mile," contrasting with larger impersonal institutions. Membership is restricted to those with Latvian organizational affiliations or family connections to members, creating a tight-knit community feel. The credit union operates extended hours (9am-6pm on Wednesdays) and maintains a single physical location in Minneapolis at 3152 17th Ave S.

The institution is a legitimate NCUA-insured credit union offering standard financial products with honest positioning around competitive rates and personal service. However, prospective members should note the limited membership eligibility requirements, single physical location, and modest service hours compared to larger financial institutions. The credit union appears well-maintained and actively serving its community, with modern online/mobile capabilities including a branded app and remote deposit features.
services: Interest-bearing checking accounts with no minimum balance
Teen VISA debit accounts with surcharge-free ATM access (24,000 locations)
pros: NCUA-insured credit union with 60+ years of operational history and stability
Membership includes access to 24,000 surcharge-free ATMs through VISA debit card network
No minimum balance requirement on interest-bearing checking accounts
Extended Wednesday hours (9am-6pm) for working members
Remote deposit capability available through mobile app for check deposits
Teen debit accounts with VISA card to build financial independence early
Personalized service model with emphasis on one-on-one assistance
cons: Restrictive membership eligibility based on Latvian organizational affiliation or family connection—not open to general public
Single physical location in Minneapolis limits accessibility for non-local members
Limited hours outside Wednesday (9am-4pm Mon/Tue/Thu/Fri) may not suit all working schedules
No mention of online account opening or membership application processing
Limited information on specific loan rates, terms, or APR ranges published on website
best_for: Members of Latvian organizations (LOAM, Latvian Evangelical Lutheran Church, Daugavas Vanagi) seeking personalized community banking
Families with Latvian heritage or relatives previously connected to the credit union
Minneapolis-area residents wanting relationship-based banking with extended Wednesday hours
Individuals seeking no-minimum-balance interest-bearing checking with competitive loan rates
diagnosis: Latvian Credit Union is best for Minneapolis-area members of the Latvian community or their relatives who value personalized, relationship-based banking and competitive loan rates. The primary caveat is restrictive membership eligibility—this institution is not open to the general public and requires affiliation with specific Latvian organizations or family connection to existing members.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
